concepts.title: Admission of a Partner
concepts.description: When a partner retires, dies, or is removed from the firm, the remaining partners have the option to admit a new partner. The new partner acquires a share in the profits and losses of the firm from the date of admission.

concepts.title: Revaluation of Assets and Liabilities
concepts.description: Revaluation of assets and liabilities is the process of revaluing the assets and liabilities of the firm at their current market value. This is done to reflect the changes in the value of assets and liabilities due to external factors such as changes in market value or inflation.

concepts.title: Calculation of Goodwill
concepts.description: Goodwill is the excess of the value of the firm's assets over the total value of its liabilities. It can be calculated using the following formula: Goodwill = (Capital Reserves + Profit + Accumulated Losses) - (Value of Assets - Value of Liabilities)

concepts.title: Order of Procedure for Admission of a Partner
concepts.description: 'R.A.C.E.' - Revaluation of Assets and Liabilities, Adjusting Capitals, and Calculating the New Partner's Share

concepts.title: Dissolution of Partnership
concepts.description: Dissolution of partnership means the winding up of the partnership firm and the sharing of its assets and liabilities among its partners.

concepts.title: Ignoring Revaluation of Assets and Liabilities
concepts.description: Students often ignore the revaluation of assets and liabilities while admission of a partner. This can lead to incorrect calculation of goodwill and capitals of partners.

concepts.title: Calculation of New Partner's Share
concepts.description: The new partner's share is calculated by multiplying the new partner's capital by the ratio of the new partner's capital to the total capital of the firm.

concepts.title: Capital Accounts of Partners
concepts.description: Capital accounts of partners are the accounts that show the capital contributed by each partner, including the share of profits and losses.
